target.name: brevicarine
target.motivation: Brevicarine (2) exhibits several pharmacological activities: it acts as an antioxidant, shows antibacterial activity against Mycobacterium tuberculosis, has antiproliferative effects against triple-negative breast cancer, serves as an agent against Parkinson's disease, and possesses skin anti-inflammatory properties. The dihydrochloride salt has been tested in vivo as an antiarrhythmic agent, demonstrating superior efficacy compared to quinidine and novocainamide. Research on the total synthesis of these alkaloids and their closely related derivatives is crucial for further confirming their structure and for ensuring their accessibility for pharmacological measurements.

prior_syntheses.key_strategy: Semi-synthetic access from brevicolline ((S)-1) isolated from natural sources, involving pyrrolidine ring opening, N-benzoylation, debenzoylation, and catalytic hydrogenation of a C=C double bond.
Condensation of indole with 1-methylpiperidone, N-alkylation, ring-opening, Beckmann rearrangement, cyclization, dehydrogenation, and debenzylation.
Treatment of nitrovinylindole with N-methylpyrrole, catalytic hydrogenation of pyrrole ring and nitro group, N-acetylation, and cyclization.

success_criteria.free_text: Develop a novel, scalable synthesis of brevicarine (2) and fully assign NMR data for both the base and its dihydrochloride salt.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis of brevicarine (2) started with the known synthesis of 5 from 3, followed by catalytic reduction of the triple bond of phthaloyl intermediate 5 to give compound 24. Removal of the phthalimide group with methylamine resulted in amine 25. The methyl group was introduced by N-formylation of the primary amine group of 25 to give congener 26, followed by reduction of the formyl group with borane–dimethyl sulfide complex to result in brevicarine (2).

thought.background: A new, versatile key triflate intermediate 3 was developed for the synthesis of racemic brevicolline ((±)-1), allowing the introduction of substituents attached by a C–C bond to position 4 of the β-carboline scaffold by cross-coupling reactions.
thought.gap: The current work aims to apply this strategy to the synthesis of brevicarine (2).
thought.decision: To introduce the side chain, a Sonogashira reaction of compound 3 with N-(3-butynyl)phthalimide (4) was chosen, expecting to form the coupled compound 5.

outcome.observation: Sonogashira reaction of compound 3 with N-(3-butynyl)phthalimide (4) led to coupled compound 5 in excellent yield.

evidence.text_span: The synthesis of brevicarine (2) (Scheme 5) started with the known synthesis of 5 from 3, followed by catalytic reduction of the triple bond of phthaloyl intermediate 5 to give compound 24.

thought.background: Intermediate 5, containing a triple bond and a phthalimide protecting group, has been successfully synthesized.
thought.gap: The next step is to reduce the triple bond to a saturated chain.
thought.decision: Catalytic reduction of the triple bond of phthaloyl intermediate 5 was chosen to give compound 24.

outcome.observation: Catalytic reduction of the triple bond of phthaloyl intermediate 5 gave compound 24.

evidence.text_span: The synthesis of brevicarine (2) (Scheme 5) started with the known synthesis of 5 from 3, followed by catalytic reduction of the triple bond of phthaloyl intermediate 5 to give compound 24.

thought.background: Compound 24, containing a phthalimide protecting group, has been synthesized.
thought.gap: The phthalimide group needs to be removed to reveal the primary amine.
thought.decision: Removal of the phthalimide group with methylamine was chosen to result in amine 25.

outcome.observation: Removal of the phthalimide group with methylamine resulted in amine 25.

evidence.text_span: Removal of the phthalimide group with methylamine resulted in amine 25.

thought.background: Amine 25 is a key intermediate for brevicarine. Brevicarine has a methyl group on the nitrogen.
thought.gap: A method for N-monomethylation of the primary amino group of compound 25 is needed.
thought.decision: Literature review was conducted for N-monomethylation methods.

outcome.observation: The authors considered alkylation with methyl iodide or Eschweiler–Clarke reductive amination with formaldehyde and formic acid.

evidence.text_span: Our experiments for the N-monomethylation of the primary amino group of compound 25 by alkylation with methyl iodide or by Eschweiler–Clarke reductive amination with formaldehyde and formic acid were unsuccessful, because the dimethylated byproduct was also formed, even when one equivalent alkylating agent was used.

thought.decision: Alkylation with methyl iodide was attempted for N-monomethylation of the primary amino group of compound 25.

outcome.observation: Alkylation with methyl iodide was unsuccessful because the dimethylated byproduct was also formed, even when one equivalent alkylating agent was used.

evidence.text_span: Our experiments for the N-monomethylation of the primary amino group of compound 25 by alkylation with methyl iodide or by Eschweiler–Clarke reductive amination with formaldehyde and formic acid were unsuccessful, because the dimethylated byproduct was also formed, even when one equivalent alkylating agent was used.

thought.decision: Eschweiler–Clarke reductive amination with formaldehyde and formic acid was attempted for N-monomethylation of the primary amino group of compound 25.

outcome.observation: Eschweiler–Clarke reductive amination was unsuccessful because the dimethylated byproduct was also formed, even when one equivalent alkylating agent was used.

evidence.text_span: Our experiments for the N-monomethylation of the primary amino group of compound 25 by alkylation with methyl iodide or by Eschweiler–Clarke reductive amination with formaldehyde and formic acid were unsuccessful, because the dimethylated byproduct was also formed, even when one equivalent alkylating agent was used.

thought.background: Direct N-monomethylation of amine 25 led to overmethylation.
thought.gap: A strategy to completely avoid overmethylation is needed.
thought.decision: To completely avoid the possibility of overmethylation, the methyl group was introduced by N-formylation of the primary amine group of 25 to give congener 26, followed by reduction of the formyl group.

evidence.text_span: Finally, our efforts were crowned by success. In order to completely avoid the possibility of overmethylation [37], the methyl group was introduced by N-formylation of the primary amine group of 25 to give congener 26, followed by reduction of the formyl group with borane–dimethyl sulfide complex [38] to result in brevicarine (2, isolated as its dihydrochloride salt).

thought.gap: A method to introduce a single methyl group without overmethylation is needed.
thought.decision: N-formylation of the primary amine group of 25 was chosen to give congener 26.

outcome.observation: N-formylation of the primary amine group of 25 gave congener 26.

evidence.text_span: In order to completely avoid the possibility of overmethylation [37], the methyl group was introduced by N-formylation of the primary amine group of 25 to give congener 26, followed by reduction of the formyl group with borane–dimethyl sulfide complex [38] to result in brevicarine (2, isolated as its dihydrochloride salt).

thought.gap: The formyl group needs to be reduced to a methyl group to obtain brevicarine.
thought.decision: Reduction of the formyl group with borane–dimethyl sulfide complex was chosen to result in brevicarine (2).

outcome.observation: Reduction of the formyl group with borane–dimethyl sulfide complex resulted in brevicarine (2), isolated as its dihydrochloride salt.

evidence.text_span: In order to completely avoid the possibility of overmethylation [37], the methyl group was introduced by N-formylation of the primary amine group of 25 to give congener 26, followed by reduction of the formyl group with borane–dimethyl sulfide complex [38] to result in brevicarine (2, isolated as its dihydrochloride salt).

thought.gap: Full structural confirmation and assignment of NMR signals are needed, as previous reports were incomplete.
thought.decision: Detailed NMR studies were conducted to assign the NMR signals for both the base and its dihydrochloride salt.

outcome.observation: The NMR data of the synthesized brevicarine (2) base and dihydrochloride salt were fully assigned. The mentioned signals from rudimentary 1H NMR spectrum reported in 1969 are identical to those of our synthetic product.

evidence.text_span: The NMR data of our synthesized brevicarine (2) base and dihydrochloride salt are summarized in Table 1. Although a rudimentary 1H NMR spectrum of isolated brevicarine (2) base and some of the NMR signals were reported in 1969 [39], the signals were not fully assigned. However, the mentioned signals are identical to those of our synthetic product. All other publications [19,23,24] confirmed the structure with IR and MS data, or by reactivity. Herein, we report the full set of assigned 1H and 13C NMR data for both the base and the dihydrochloride salt.

thought.background: Amine 25 is available. An alternative route to brevicarine (2) was explored.
thought.gap: A different method for N-methylation is desired.
thought.decision: Carbamate 28 was prepared from amine 25 by ethoxycarbonylation, with the expectation to reduce it to brevicarine (2) using LiAlH4.

outcome.observation: The reduction stopped at the N-formyl (26) stage; the formation of brevicarine (2) could not be detected by LC–MS.

evidence.text_span: Based on literature data, we expected to transform carbamate 28, obtained from amine 25 by ethoxycarbonylation, into brevicarine (2) by reduction with LiAlH4 [40,41]. However, to our surprise, the reduction stopped at the N-formyl (26) stage, the formation of brevicarine (2) could not be detected by LC–MS.

validation.significance: A new method for the synthesis of β-carboline alkaloid brevicarine has been elaborated, rendering the preparation of larger amounts of the target compound possible. NMR data of brevicarine base and dihydrochloride salt were fully assigned for a further confirmation of their structure. This novel approach ensures a broad accessibility to the target compound for potential pharmacological measurements.

source.title: Studies on the syntheses of β-carboline alkaloids brevicarine and brevicolline.
source.authors: Batizi B, Pollák P, Dancsó A, Keglevich P, Simig G, Volk B, Milen M.
